what_it_is: Full-body two-stage skill router evaluated at 80K scale.
architecture: broad embedding retrieval then full-content rerank
surprises: body ablation loses 29–44 reported points
current Ingot embeds descriptions only

what_it_is: Explainable hybrid skill router and graph atlas.
architecture: SQLite bundle index with weighted score components and low-confidence abstention
surprises: default weights are static rather than learned
relation vocabulary maps cleanly to empirical CARN edges

architecture: offline graph plus online hybrid seed and graph rerank
surprises: reverse edges use asymmetric weights
graph value depends on trustworthy edges

what_it_is: Governed skill foundry with canonical sources and runtime projections.
architecture: canonical source to hashed manifest to budgeted generated projections
surprises: three-observation promotion heuristic
several mechanisms are contracts rather than proven product behavior

what_it_is: Self-evolving routed skill library and market.
architecture: read-execute-reflect-write loop over local and remote registries
surprises: closest product overlap
framework code is not licensed despite individually licensed bundled skills

what_it_is: Typed contract graph and maintenance loops for skill libraries.
architecture: task-time stitching and repair separated from library-time merge, retire, and propagation
surprises: best shared representation for Ingot and CARN
large-scale claims include synthetic degraded skills

what_it_is: Bounded hierarchy of isolated autonomous agent loops.
surprises: useful for runtime hierarchy, not skill composition
reserves cleanup budget

what_it_is: Portable record/replay and counterfactual regression system.
architecture: call-site canonicalization plus strict event sequence and optional SQLite index
surprises: counterfactual forks hardlink blobs
cannot isolate unwrapped side effects

what_it_is: Deterministic Starlark runtime with host-call recording.
architecture: small host boundary stores observed results and replays without model calls
surprises: resume is replay-to-suspension
minimal contract fits a first vertical slice

what_it_is: Offline agent-run recording and first-divergence comparison.
architecture: run/event store plus deterministic compare pipeline
surprises: closer to regression comparison than an action cache
operator diagnostics are stronger than execution reuse

what_it_is: Event-sourced agent graph with replay, prefix forks, and structural promotion.
architecture: immutable event log with derived graph and fork-local tails
surprises: strongest event-prefix lineage model
direct unwrapped effects still violate determinism

what_it_is: Durable workflow history and replay-compatibility reference.
architecture: workflow code re-executes against an append-only history and must reproduce commands
surprises: release history replay maps directly to Ingot evidence
reset creates new execution from a valid prefix boundary

what_it_is: Database-backed durable workflow checkpoints and forks.
architecture: function restarts from input and consumes outputs by deterministic step identity
surprises: copy-on-fork prefix is a compact counterfactual primitive
source hash prevents incompatible recovery

what_it_is: Mobile trajectory compiler and validated replay system.
architecture: regex then embedding then app-filter match, followed by stepwise guarded replay
license: paper only; no reusable software license found
surprises: strongest complete replay design
abstract contains an apparent 1000% typo

what_it_is: Cache of successful parameterized agent plans.
architecture: correct trace to concise plan template to retrieval and adaptation
license: paper only; no reusable implementation found
surprises: lower fuzzy thresholds trade accuracy for hits and cost
no environment or runtime validator

architecture: recurring class advances through evidence tiers and falls back on drift
surprises: best control-plane framing
reported thresholds should not become universal defaults

what_it_is: Portkey, LangChain Redis, and GPTCache semantic response caching patterns.
architecture: candidate retrieval by prompt similarity plus configuration key
surprises: Portkey ignores system prompt for cache matching
response-cache identity is far too weak for actions

what_it_is: Probabilistic Language Tries, SGLang, and LMCache shared-prefix reuse patterns.
architecture: retain reusable prefixes and recompute divergence-sensitive residuals
surprises: exact prefix systems are safe because identity is structural
semantic action reuse requires an additional validation plane
